name: IoMT Medical Device Compromise
description: Compromised IoMT device (infusion pump) on VLAN 40 starts lateral scanning. Triggers IPS alerts on medical device VLAN, anomalous traffic patterns, and potential patient safety concern if device reaches HL7 interface.

events.description: FHIR API calls from unexpected IP range (10.10.40.x — medical devices shouldn't call FHIR)
Malformed HL7 messages from unexpected source
Interface engine rejecting messages from unknown sender

network_correlation.description: Infusion pump on VLAN 40 (IoMT) begins ARP scanning, DNS lookups to C2 domain, and lateral movement attempts to VLAN 30 (HL7) and VLAN 10 (Epic app tier)

events.description: Port security detects unusual MAC activity on infusion pump port
IoMT device making DNS queries to external resolver — should use internal only
Firewall denies MLLP (HL7) connection attempt from IoMT device
Firewall denies HTTPS connection to Interconnect from IoMT device
Palo Alto detects lateral scanning from IoMT device
Security team shuts down the compromised device port

flow_anomalies.description: ARP scan on IoMT VLAN — device enumerating neighbors
Attempted lateral movement to HL7 VLAN
Attempted lateral movement to Epic app tier

indicators: IoMT device (10.10.40.x) should NEVER make outbound DNS to external resolvers
IoMT device attempting to connect to HL7 port (2575) is a critical indicator
Port security violation precedes all other events — earliest detection
Micro-segmentation (firewall deny rules) successfully contains the compromise
ARP flood pattern on VLAN 40 visible in NetFlow before threat detection
